llm.system_prompt: You are a cybersecurity and code analysis expert with deep knowledge in vulnerability detection, secure coding practices, and static code analysis.

benign_code.description: A benign source code function in which to inject a vulnerability.

vul_inject_id.description: Id of the vulnerability id associated with the benign code in PrimeVul. Must belong to available_vul_categories.

benign_code.description: A benign source code function in which to inject a vulnerability.

vul_inject_id.description: Id of the vulnerability id associated with the benign code in PrimeVul. Must belong to available_vul_categories.

function_purpose.description: A concise summary of what the function is intended to do.

input_descriptions.description: Description of the expected inputs, including assumed data types and formats.

output_descriptions.description: Description of the expected output, including assumed data type and format.

source_sink_list.description: List of sources and sinks where potential vulnerabilities could be inserted.

data_flows.description: Description of how data flows between sources and sinks.

benign_code.description: A benign source code function in which to inject a vulnerability.

vul_inject_id.description: Id of the vulnerability id associated with the benign code in PrimeVul. Must belong to available_vul_categories.

vul_inject_info.description: Information about the category of code vulnerability to inject, ready to be used in prompt.

benign_code.description: A benign source code function in which to inject a vulnerability.

vul_inject_id.description: Id of the vulnerability id associated with the benign code in PrimeVul. Must belong to available_vul_categories.

vul_inject_info.description: Information about the category of code vulnerability to inject, ready to be used in prompt.

function_purpose.description: A concise summary of what the function is intended to do.

input_descriptions.description: Description of the expected inputs, including assumed data types and formats.

output_descriptions.description: Description of the expected output, including assumed data type and format.

source_sink_list.description: List of sources and sinks in between which potential vulnerabilities could be inserted.

data_flows.description: Description of how data flows between sources and sinks.

sanitization.description: Sanitization and vulnerability mitigation elements that makes the code benign to vul_inject_id.

vulnerable_code.description: The modified version of the benign function containing the injected vulnerability.

injection_location.description: A clear indication of where in the function the vulnerability was injected (e.g., line number, code block description, or section of the function).

injection_justification.description: A detailed explanation of why this vulnerability fits naturally within the benign function, referencing the analysis of the benign function, the retrieved vulnerable context, and the vulnerability injection information.

confidence_score.description: Score between 0 and 10 on how confident you are that your answer is correct and the vulnerable code actually is vulnerable with the proper vulnerability.
